label: Record Details
description: Counterparty, source reference, and fee fields for record-only booking mode.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary wraps all widgets globally in widget-wrapper.tsx — confirmed present.

0.4.note: No `any` types or @ts-ignore in widget file.

0.5.note: No lib/mocks/ imports in widget file. All state sourced from useBookTradeData() context hook.

0.6.note: Pure data-entry form — counterparty, sourceReference, and fee are controlled-state strings from context with no async load path. No loading state applicable.

0.7.note: When executionMode !== 'record_only', widget renders 'Switch to Record Only mode for counterparty and fee fields.' guidance message — correct empty/inactive-mode state.

0.8.note: This widget is a pure input form — it does not perform or trigger async operations. Order submission errors are handled by book-preview-compliance-widget which owns the submit state machine. No error state applicable here.

0.9.note: No commented-out code, dead branches, or unreachable stubs found.

l0.notes: 7 checks pass, 2 N/A (0.6 and 0.8 — pure synchronous input form with no async path). No fixes required.

1.1.note: executionMode, counterparty, setCounterparty, sourceReference, setSourceReference, fee, setFee all sourced exclusively via useBookTradeData(). No inline data fetch or direct imports.

1.2.note: book-data-context.tsx calls useGlobalScope() and syncs org/client/strategy from global scope. This widget does not consume scope-filtered data directly — it provides form inputs that flow into the order payload which does use scope.

1.3.note: Record details widget contains field inputs only (counterparty, sourceReference, fee) — these do not vary by org filter. Scope changes affect orgId/clientId in the order payload (handled in context), not these fields.

1.4.note: No client-level data filtering applicable to a pure input form.

1.5.note: No strategy-level data filtering applicable to a pure input form.

1.6.note: No filterable data set — widget is a form, not a data display.

1.7.note: Widget imports no reference data. No constants or config needed — all state is free-text user input.

l1.notes: Widget is a pure input form. 1.1 and 1.2 pass. 1.3–1.6 are N/A — no filterable data. Scope changes cascade through context state into the order payload, not directly into these input fields.
